title: Global Social Sciences Postdoctoral & Fellowship Opportunities 2026: Top Fully Funded and High-Impact Research Positions Across Europe and the UK
summary: The academic and research landscape in 2026 continues to offer outstanding opportunities for early-career scholars in the social sciences , particularly in fields such as sociology, political science, anthropology, economics, gender studies, migration studies, artificial intelligence ethics, and public health. This comprehensive article highlights a curated list of active postdoctoral and fellowship opportunities across the United Kingdom, Switzerland, Sweden, Austria, and broader Europe . These positions are ideal for researchers aiming to build international academic careers, contribute to cutting-edge research, and collaborate with world-leading institutions. All opportunities listed below are currently open, with application deadlines extending into 2026 or operating on a rolling basis. Postdoctoral Fellowship – Gender Studies (Sweden) Karlstad University is recruiting a postdoctoral researcher in gender studies , focusing on gender equality, identity, and social transformation. Key features: Research on gender, society, and policy Strong academic collaboration opportunities Contribution to feminist and social theory research Application: Deadline: 15 August 2026 Apply here Postdoctoral Position – Sociology of Health STS (Switzerland) SUPSI (University of Applied Sciences and Arts of Southern Switzerland) offers a postdoctoral position in sociology of health and science and technology studies (STS) . Key features: Focus on health systems and science-society relations Applied social science research environment Strong interdisciplinary collaboration Application: Deadline: 23 August 2026 Apply here Postdoctoral Fellowship – Migration Studies (ERC Project, Austria) The University for Continuing Education Krems is hiring a postdoctoral researcher in migration studies , funded under an ERC research project. Key features: Research on migration, mobility, and integration Participation in a European Research Council (ERC) project Strong international research collaboration Application: Deadline: 24 August 2026 Apply here Post-Doctoral Programme – Social Sciences (European Scientific Institute) This program offers structured postdoctoral training and research opportunities across multiple social science disciplines. Key features: Interdisciplinary social science research program Academic mentorship and career development International collaboration opportunities Application: Deadline: 5 September 2026 Apply here MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 (European Union) The Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships are one of the largest research funding schemes in Europe. Key features: €399 million funding pool Approximately 1,600 fellowships awarded Mobility across EU and associated countries Support for innovative research projects across disciplines Application: Deadline: 9 September 2026 Apply here Postdoctoral Researcher – Social Science, AI Ethics (Luxembourg) The University of Luxembourg offers a rolling postdoctoral position focusing on the intersection of social sciences, artificial intelligence, and ethics . Key features: Research on AI governance and ethics Interdisciplinary academic environment Rolling applications accepted year-round Application: Deadline: Rolling Apply here Summary of Opportunities These fellowships and postdoctoral positions provide: Access to world-class research institutions across Europe and the UK Funding for independent academic research Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration Strong career pathways into academia, policy research, and international organizations Exposure to global research networks in social sciences Application Strategy Tips To increase your chances of success: Tailor your research proposal to each institution’s focus area Highlight interdisciplinary experience Demonstrate strong publication or research potential Align your project with European research priorities (migration, inequality, AI ethics, health, gender studies) Secure strong academic references early Learn More More global opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Global Social Sciences Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities 2026: Top Fully Funded and High-Impact Research Positions Across Europe and the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026: Google Opens Applications for African AI Startups and Researchers to Access Early Google DeepMind Models, Technical Mentorship, and Potential Funding
summary: Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries across the globe, and Africa is increasingly emerging as a hub for AI-driven innovation. To support the continent s next generation of technology pioneers, Google has officially launched applications for the Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026 —a groundbreaking initiative designed to accelerate African AI startups and researchers building solutions for uniquely African challenges. Hosted at the Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) in Ghana, the Google Africa Applied AI Lab provides founders and researchers with exclusive early access to Google DeepMind s cutting-edge AI models, personalized technical mentorship from Google experts, commercialization support, venture capital connections, and opportunities for potential funding. The initiative aims to bridge the gap between world-class AI research and commercially successful products, helping build Africa s first generation of AI-native unicorn companies. Applications are now open and will close on 31 August 2026 . What is the Google Africa Applied AI Lab? The Google Africa Applied AI Lab is an innovation and commercialization platform established by Google Labs through the Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) in Ghana, with support from the Google AI Futures Fund . Rather than functioning as a traditional accelerator or incubator, the programme focuses on collaborative product development between Google experts and selected African startups. Its primary objective is to help founders transform promising AI ideas into scalable, market-ready products capable of solving significant challenges across Africa. The programme connects entrepreneurs directly with: Google Research experts Google DeepMind engineers Product specialists Venture capital investors AI researchers Commercialization experts Participants receive tailored technical support based on the maturity and needs of their startup. The Mission of the Google Africa Applied AI Lab The programme seeks to: Accelerate AI innovation across Africa. Support commercialization of breakthrough AI technologies. Build globally competitive African AI startups. Connect founders with world-class AI expertise. Encourage responsible AI development. Strengthen Africa s AI ecosystem. Foster collaboration between research and entrepreneurship. Ultimately, the initiative aims to position Africa as a major contributor to the future of artificial intelligence. Where is the Programme Based? The Google Africa Applied AI Lab is headquartered at the: Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) Location: Accra, Ghana The AICC serves as one of Africa s leading AI innovation hubs, providing collaborative workspaces, community programming, and technical support to entrepreneurs and researchers across the continent. Although the programme culminates in an in-person event in Ghana, participants are not required to remain physically based at the Centre throughout the programme . Focus Areas of the Google Africa Applied AI Lab Google is looking for AI-powered products capable of addressing meaningful challenges across Africa. The programme focuses on five major technological futures expected to shape the continent over the coming years. 1. The Future of Work Innovations that improve productivity, employment, workforce development, automation, and digital labor. Examples include: Workforce AI tools HR technology Employment platforms AI productivity assistants 2. The Future of Knowledge Solutions that improve how people create, access, organize, and distribute knowledge. Examples include: Education technology Knowledge management AI tutoring systems Information accessibility 3. The Future of Software Development Products that enhance software engineering using AI. Possible innovations include: AI coding assistants Development automation Software testing Programming productivity tools 4. The Future of Creativity Projects supporting creative industries through AI. Examples include: Design tools Creative assistants AI-generated content Digital art solutions 5. The Future of Entertainment Applications using AI to transform media and entertainment. Examples include: Gaming Interactive storytelling Video creation Music technologies Benefits of the Google Africa Applied AI Lab Selected startups gain access to a comprehensive package of technical, commercial, and investment support. 1. Early Access to Google DeepMind AI Models Participants receive early access to Google s newest AI technologies before they become publicly available. Available models may include: Gemini Gemma Veo This gives startups a competitive advantage in developing innovative commercial AI products. 2. Technical Mentorship Founders work closely with: Google Research Google engineering teams Google AI specialists Mentorship includes: Product architecture AI implementation Technical guidance Model optimization 3. Commercialization Support The programme helps startups move from research and prototypes to scalable businesses. Support includes: Product validation Market strategy Business development Go-to-market planning 4. Venture Capital Mentorship Participants receive mentorship from leading venture capital firms, including: 4DX Ventures Norrsken22 Novastar Ventures Ventures Platform These firms provide guidance on: Scaling startups Fundraising Investor readiness Business strategy 5. Potential Funding Opportunities Selected startups may also become eligible for investment opportunities through: Google AI Futures Fund Partner venture capital firms Funding decisions are made separately and participation in the programme does not automatically guarantee investment. 6. Product Demo Day The programme concludes with an in-person Demo Day at the Accra AI Community Centre. Participants will present their AI products to: Google teams Investors Venture capital firms Strategic partners Industry experts This provides valuable visibility and networking opportunities. 7. No Equity Requirement One of the programme s most attractive features is that participating startups do not give up equity or ownership simply by joining the programme . However, founders may later receive investment offers from Google AI Futures Fund or participating venture capital firms. Programme Timeline The Google Africa Applied AI Lab follows a structured timeline. Applications Open 1 July 2026 Application Deadline 31 August 2026 Selection Notifications September 2026 Co-development Period Mid-September 2026 to Early December 2026 Demo Day Early December 2026 Programme Structure The programme consists of a collaborative co-development phase rather than classroom-based training. Support is customized for each startup based on: Product maturity Technical needs Commercial goals Market readiness The formal programme ends after Demo Day, but participants can continue accessing support through the Accra AI Community Centre. Who Can Apply? Google welcomes applications from individuals and teams building AI solutions for Africa. Eligible applicants include: Full-time startup founders AI entrepreneurs Researchers planning to become founders AI innovators Technology builders Applicants should have ambitious ideas capable of generating significant impact across Africa. Startup Stage Eligibility The programme accepts startups across multiple stages, including: Idea stage Pre-Seed Seed Series A Series B Series C+ Although startups with commercial traction receive preference, applicants with promising ideas are also encouraged to apply. Does Your Startup Need to Use Google AI? Yes. Applicants must demonstrate that Google AI technologies will play a meaningful role within their product. However: There is no exclusivity requirement . Teams may continue using AI models from other providers alongside Google AI technologies. Do You Need a Co-Founder? No. Solo founders are welcome to apply. However, Google evaluates team composition as part of the selection process. A well-balanced team may strengthen an application. Do You Need to Be Technical? Not necessarily. Applicants do not need to be Google AI experts. However, technical capability remains an important evaluation criterion because selected teams must successfully develop AI-powered products. Selection Process After applications close: Google reviews submissions. Applications are assessed against programme priorities. Additional information may be requested. Selected applicants are notified beginning in September 2026. Applicants who are unsuccessful will be notified, although Google notes that it cannot provide individual feedback. Venture Capital Partners The programme collaborates with leading investment firms, including: 4DX Ventures Norrsken22 Novastar Ventures Ventures Platform Google AI Futures Fund These organizations provide: Mentorship Strategic advice Networking Potential investment opportunities About the Accra AI Community Centre The Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) is an AI innovation hub located in Accra, Ghana. Its mission is to: Strengthen Africa s AI ecosystem. Support responsible AI innovation. Reduce AI skills gaps. Foster collaboration among entrepreneurs and researchers. Provide a shared technology and innovation space. The Google Africa Applied AI Lab operates from this centre. About the Google AI Futures Fund The Google AI Futures Fund (AIFF) supports innovators building the next generation of AI technologies. The fund provides: Capital Early access to Google DeepMind models Distribution opportunities Strategic partnerships Long-term support for breakthrough AI companies Some participants in the Google Africa Applied AI Lab may become eligible for investment through AIFF. Why You Should Apply The Google Africa Applied AI Lab represents one of the continent s most exciting AI commercialization opportunities. Participants gain: Early access to advanced Google AI models Direct collaboration with Google experts Personalized technical mentorship Commercialization guidance Venture capital exposure Potential funding opportunities Investor Demo Day Access to Africa s leading AI innovation hub Continued support beyond the programme Whether you are building AI for education, healthcare, finance, software, creativity, productivity, or entertainment, this initiative provides an exceptional platform to accelerate your startup. How to Apply Interested founders and researchers should submit their applications through the official Google Africa Applied AI Lab application portal. Applicants should prepare: Startup information Product description AI use case Team details Commercialization plan Vision for impact across Africa Applications must be submitted before the deadline. APPLY HERE Application Deadline 31 August 2026 Applications opened on 1 July 2026 , and selected applicants will begin receiving notifications in September 2026 . Click HERE for more information about Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026. Conclusion The Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026 is a landmark initiative designed to empower Africa s next generation of AI entrepreneurs and researchers. By combining Google DeepMind s cutting-edge technologies with expert mentorship, commercialization support, venture capital access, and potential funding opportunities, the programme offers an unparalleled environment for building impactful AI solutions tailored to African realities. Hosted at the Accra AI Community Centre in Ghana, the programme goes beyond traditional startup acceleration by fostering close collaboration between founders and Google experts while connecting participants with leading investors and strategic partners. For ambitious innovators seeking to transform groundbreaking ideas into scalable businesses capable of shaping Africa s digital future, the Google Africa Applied AI Lab represents an exceptional opportunity. Interested founders and researchers are encouraged to prepare strong applications and submit them before the 31 August 2026 deadline. Discover more global Fellowship , AI , and African opportunities on OFY. The post Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026: Google Opens Applications for African AI Startups and Researchers to Access Early Google DeepMind Models, Technical Mentorship, and Potential Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Paid CRIS Research Internship 2026 in France: Apply for a 4-Month Research Internship at Sciences Po on Labor Market Inequalities
summary: If you are a Master s student interested in social science research, labor market inequalities, computational methods, data analysis, and programming, the Centre for Research on social InequalitieS (CRIS) at Sciences Po is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity beginning in September 2026 . This four-month, part-time internship provides an excellent opportunity to work alongside experienced researchers on a cutting-edge project that explores how skills influence labor market opportunities and inequalities. Interns will gain practical research experience, strengthen their analytical abilities, and contribute to a major academic research project using one of the largest datasets of online job advertisements. The application deadline is 20 July 2026 at 9:00 AM CET . About the Centre for Research on Social Inequalities (CRIS) The Centre for Research on social InequalitieS (CRIS) is a joint research center established by Sciences Po and CNRS (French National Centre for Scientific Research) . CRIS is internationally recognized for producing high-quality, theory-driven empirical research on social inequalities. The center brings together: 22 permanent researchers 6 emeritus researchers 29 PhD students A dedicated six-member support team Researchers at CRIS investigate inequality from multiple perspectives using interdisciplinary approaches and a wide variety of research methods, including: Qualitative research Quantitative research Experimental methods Comparative research Micro-level analysis Meso-level analysis Macro-level analysis Their research contributes to both academic knowledge and evidence-based policymaking. Research Areas at CRIS CRIS researchers specialize in numerous areas related to social inequalities, including: Education Labor market inequalities Economic inequality Social mobility Welfare states Public policy Urban segregation Migration Discrimination Social movements Policing Cultural consumption Digital inequalities Health inequalities Environmental inequalities Life course dynamics The center emphasizes: Empirical research Methodological rigor Interdisciplinary collaboration International perspectives Comparative research Theoretical pluralism About the Internship Project The internship forms part of an ongoing research project led by Marie Labussière . The project seeks to better understand how workers skills influence labor market opportunities. Although educational qualifications and occupations are commonly used to study labor markets, individual skills themselves have received comparatively less direct attention. This research aims to fill that gap by measuring skills directly through large-scale analysis of online job advertisements. Using computational approaches and advanced data analysis techniques, the project investigates how different skills are valued across occupations and how they contribute to labor market inequalities. What Will Interns Do? Successful applicants will work closely with the project supervisor and contribute to several important research activities. Primary responsibilities include: Conducting literature reviews on labor market inequalities and skills research. Analyzing large datasets of online job advertisements. Coding latent skill dimensions. Performing both manual coding and computational coding. Working with word embedding models. Supporting ongoing academic research activities. Throughout the internship, participants will receive guidance from the project supervisor while developing valuable research and analytical skills in an academic environment. Skills You Will Develop This internship offers practical experience in several highly valuable areas, including: Academic research Literature review Data analysis Statistical analysis Computational social science Programming Python applications Word embedding models Labor market research Research methodology Analytical thinking Quantitative research methods These skills are highly relevant for students considering careers in academia, research institutions, policy organizations, international organizations, or data-driven social science. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following requirements: Educational Background Applicants must be currently pursuing a Master s degree in one of the following fields: Sociology Economics Psychology Computational Social Sciences Technical Skills Candidates should have: Statistical knowledge Experience with regression analysis Basic knowledge of linear algebra, including vector projections Programming proficiency Knowledge of Python is considered an advantage. However, applicants with strong motivation to learn Python are also encouraged to apply. Internship Duration The internship will run for: Duration: 4 months Period: September to December 2026 Working Hours The internship is part-time and requires: 18 hours per week This makes it suitable for students who wish to combine research experience with their academic commitments. Internship Benefits Successful interns will receive numerous professional and academic benefits, including: Paid internship Hands-on research experience Mentorship from an experienced academic researcher Experience working with large real-world datasets Exposure to computational research methods Development of statistical and programming skills Opportunity to contribute to impactful research on labor market inequalities Experience within an internationally respected research center Strong addition to an academic CV Compensation Interns will receive: Internship allowance at the legal minimum rate applicable under French law. Internship Location Administrative Address: 27 rue Saint Guillaume 75337 Paris Cedex 07 France Office Location: 1 Place Saint Thomas d Aquin 75007 Paris France Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare: A Letter of Intent A Curriculum Vitae (maximum two pages) Applications must be submitted through the official online application form. Application Deadline Deadline: 20 July 2026 at 9:00 AM CET Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official online application form and upload: Letter of Intent CV (maximum two pages) Click here to apply Click here for more information about this Why You Should Apply This internship presents an outstanding opportunity for Master s students seeking practical research experience in computational social science and labor market analysis. Participants will collaborate with leading researchers, gain exposure to advanced analytical techniques, and contribute to an important project examining how skills shape labor market inequalities. Whether you are planning a research career, pursuing a PhD, or looking to strengthen your analytical and programming skills, this internship offers valuable academic and professional experience within one of France s leading research centers. Click here for more global internship opportunities The post Paid CRIS Research Internship 2026 in France: Apply for a 4-Month Research Internship at Sciences Po on Labor Market Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Free UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course 2026: Earn a UN Certificate and Learn How to Design Inclusive Training
summary: Creating training that truly reaches everyone requires more than good content—it requires intentional, inclusive design. If you re a trainer, instructional designer, educator, project manager, or learning professional, the UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course offers a practical opportunity to build the knowledge and skills needed to make learning more accessible, equitable, and impactful. This free, self-paced online course is open to learners from around the world and helps participants integrate gender equality, intercultural sensitivity, and disability inclusion throughout every stage of the training cycle. Upon successful completion, participants receive an official UNITAR Certificate of Completion . More than 2,000 professionals from 154 countries have already completed the course this year, making it one of UNITAR s widely accessed professional learning opportunities. There is no application deadline , and registration is currently open. About the Inclusive Learning Design Course The Inclusive Learning Design Course is developed by the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) to help learning professionals design training that is accessible, inclusive, and aligned with international standards. The course supports the implementation of the UN Disability Inclusion Strategy and promotes learning environments where every participant has an equal opportunity to access, engage with, and benefit from training. Rather than treating inclusion as an afterthought, the course demonstrates how to incorporate inclusive principles into every phase of the learning process—from planning and design to delivery and evaluation. What You Will Learn Participants will gain practical knowledge and skills to: Mainstream gender equality throughout training design and delivery. Integrate disability inclusion into learning programmes. Apply intercultural sensitivity across diverse learning environments. Use inclusivity principles throughout the entire training cycle. Design accessible learning materials. Create inclusive learning environments. Apply inclusive facilitation techniques. Evaluate training programmes using inclusive approaches. Apply the Do No Harm principle to avoid unintended negative consequences during training implementation. Course Features The course has been designed to provide a flexible and engaging learning experience. Self-Paced Learning Participants can complete the course entirely online at their own convenience. Approximately 2 hours of learning time. Study whenever your schedule allows. No fixed class sessions. Interactive Learning Experience The course includes: Real-world case studies. Scenario-based learning activities. Practical exercises. Opportunities to apply inclusive design concepts immediately. Official UNITAR Certificate Participants who successfully complete the final assessment receive an official UNITAR Certificate of Completion , which can be added to professional portfolios, CVs, and LinkedIn profiles. Practical Resources Learners also gain access to downloadable resources, including: Inclusivity checklists. Job aids. Practical guidance for implementing inclusive learning design. Reference materials for future projects. Who Should Take This Course? This course is particularly valuable for professionals involved in designing, managing, or delivering learning programmes, including: Learning Specialists and Instructional Designers Learn how to integrate accessibility and inclusivity throughout the learning design process. Trainers and Facilitators Strengthen your facilitation techniques and learn how to create engaging, inclusive learning environments for diverse audiences. Project Managers and Programme Officers Ensure that training programmes align with quality standards, inclusion principles, and the UN Disability Inclusion Strategy. The course is also suitable for: Educators Learning and Development professionals NGO staff Government officials International development practitioners Human resource professionals Anyone interested in making learning more inclusive Why Take This Course? Inclusive learning benefits everyone. By participating in this course, you will: Improve the quality of your training programmes. Design learning experiences that are accessible to diverse audiences. Strengthen your professional skills. Gain internationally recognised knowledge from UNITAR. Earn an official certificate at no cost. Learn practical techniques that can be immediately applied in your workplace. Contribute to more equitable and inclusive education and professional development. Course Details Course Type: Online Cost: Free Language: English Learning Format: Self-paced Duration: Approximately 2 hours Certificate: Official UNITAR Certificate of Completion Registration: Open How to Enrol Registration is currently open, and learners can begin immediately. Start the course here Why This Course Matters As organisations increasingly prioritise accessibility, diversity, equity, and inclusion, professionals who understand inclusive learning design are becoming more valuable across sectors. Whether you work in education, government, international development, humanitarian organisations, NGOs, or the private sector, this course provides practical skills that can help you create learning experiences that are more effective, equitable, and impactful. Since there is no enrolment deadline, you can register today and begin learning immediately. Click here for more global online courses The post Free UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course 2026: Earn a UN Certificate and Learn How to Design Inclusive Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026: Up to $1.5 Million in Funding for High-Growth Small and Medium Enterprises Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America
summary: Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are widely recognized as engines of economic growth, innovation, and job creation. However, many promising businesses in emerging markets struggle to access the financing and technical support needed to expand operations, enter new markets, adopt new technologies, and build long-term resilience. To help address these challenges, the SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026 is now accepting applications. This funding opportunity provides between USD 50,000 and USD 1.5 million in grant funding, alongside mentorship, technical assistance, networking opportunities, and access to potential follow-on financing for high-potential SMEs operating across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. The programme is specifically designed to support for-profit businesses with strong growth potential that are committed to creating quality jobs, strengthening local economies, and promoting sustainable and inclusive development. Applications are currently open, and eligible SMEs are encouraged to submit their applications before the 15 November 2026 deadline. About the SME Growth Accelerator Grant The SME Growth Accelerator is a grant programme focused on supporting ambitious small and medium-sized enterprises operating in emerging markets. The programme aims to help businesses overcome common barriers to growth by providing both financial support and strategic business assistance. Rather than simply offering funding, the initiative combines capital with mentorship, technical support, and business networking to strengthen participating companies and improve their long-term competitiveness. Successful applicants will gain access to resources that can help them: Scale business operations Expand into new domestic and international markets Increase productivity Strengthen organizational capacity Improve sustainability practices Create quality employment opportunities Prepare for future investment opportunities The programme recognizes that SMEs play a vital role in driving inclusive economic growth, innovation, and sustainable development across emerging economies. Funding Available Successful applicants may receive grant funding ranging from: Minimum Funding: USD 50,000 Maximum Funding: USD 1.5 million The amount awarded will depend on the proposed project, business needs, scalability, and expected development impact. What the Grant Supports The SME Growth Accelerator provides flexible funding across several business development areas. Eligible projects may include the following: 1. Working Capital Businesses can use funding to strengthen operational capacity and support day-to-day business activities that facilitate growth. Examples include: Business expansion Operational scaling Inventory financing Growth initiatives Pilot project implementation 2. Technical Assistance and Capacity Building The programme supports initiatives that improve business performance through technical expertise and organizational development. This may include: Staff development Business process improvement Operational efficiency Management systems Organizational strengthening 3. Climate and ESG-Aligned Investments The grant encourages investments that improve environmental sustainability and responsible business practices. Examples include: Climate-smart technologies Sustainability initiatives Environmental management systems ESG implementation Resource efficiency projects 4. Market Research and Feasibility Studies Funding may also support businesses seeking to better understand new markets before expanding. Examples include: Market analysis Customer research Expansion feasibility studies Product validation Market entry planning 5. Equipment and Technology Procurement Businesses can invest in equipment and technology that improve productivity and competitiveness. Examples include: Machinery Digital technologies Business software Manufacturing equipment Productivity tools 6. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning Applicants may allocate part of the funding toward measuring project outcomes and strengthening organizational learning. Activities may include: Impact measurement Data collection Performance monitoring Learning systems Evaluation frameworks Programme Benefits The SME Growth Accelerator provides significantly more than financial support. Successful applicants will benefit from several valuable opportunities designed to strengthen long-term business success. These include: Grant Funding Businesses may receive between USD 50,000 and USD 1.5 million to implement approved growth projects. Technical Assistance Participants receive expert support that helps improve operations, strengthen internal capacity, and implement business growth strategies. Mentorship The programme connects participating SMEs with experienced mentors who provide strategic guidance throughout the growth journey. Business Networks Successful applicants gain access to valuable business networks that may create opportunities for: Partnerships Collaboration Business development Market expansion Follow-On Financing Opportunities High-performing businesses may also gain access to future financing opportunities that support continued expansion beyond the grant period. Who Can Apply? The SME Growth Accelerator is open to eligible small and medium-sized enterprises operating in emerging markets. Applicants must meet all programme eligibility requirements. Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must meet the following conditions. Business Type Applicants must be: For-profit small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) Number of Employees Eligible businesses must employ between: 10 and 250 employees Annual Revenue Applicants must demonstrate verified annual revenue between: USD 250,000 and USD 10 million Geographic Eligibility The programme is open to eligible SMEs operating within: Africa Asia Latin America Why SMEs Should Apply Many businesses possess strong products and services but lack sufficient capital and strategic support to reach their full potential. The SME Growth Accelerator addresses this challenge by combining financial resources with business development services that enable companies to grow sustainably. Participating businesses can use the programme to: Expand production Enter new markets Improve competitiveness Build organizational capacity Strengthen sustainability practices Generate employment Increase long-term resilience For SMEs with ambitious growth plans, this programme offers an opportunity to accelerate business development while contributing to broader economic growth within their countries. Application Process The application process has been designed to be simple and efficient. Applicants can complete the submission online. According to the programme information: The application takes approximately 15 minutes to complete. There are no application fees . Applications are reviewed according to established World Bank Group standards. Applicants can expect a decision within 4 to 6 weeks after submission. Interested businesses should ensure they prepare all required business information before beginning the application. APPLY HERE Important Dates Applicants should note the following timeline: Application Status: Open Application Deadline: 15 November 2026 Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. Why This Grant Matters Access to growth capital remains one of the greatest barriers facing SMEs across emerging markets. Without adequate financing, many businesses struggle to: Expand production Hire additional staff Purchase equipment Improve operational systems Enter new markets Adopt sustainable technologies The SME Growth Accelerator directly addresses these challenges by combining grant funding with mentorship, technical assistance, and networking opportunities. Its emphasis on sustainable growth, climate resilience, ESG alignment, and inclusive economic development makes it particularly valuable for businesses seeking long-term impact. By supporting SMEs that demonstrate strong growth potential, the programme contributes to job creation, economic resilience, innovation, and sustainable private-sector development across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Tips for Preparing a Strong Application To maximize the likelihood of success, applicants should: Clearly explain their business model and growth strategy. Demonstrate measurable business performance and verified annual revenue. Present a realistic budget aligned with eligible funding activities. Highlight expected social, economic, or environmental impact. Show how grant funding will contribute to sustainable business growth. Explain plans for job creation, innovation, or market expansion. Submit the application well before the closing date. Visit HERE to learn more about the SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026. Conclusion The SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for high-potential small and medium-sized enterprises across Africa, Asia, and Latin America to secure substantial funding and strategic business support. Offering grants ranging from USD 50,000 to USD 1.5 million , the programme goes beyond financial assistance by providing mentorship, technical assistance, access to professional networks, and opportunities for follow-on financing. Whether your business is looking to expand into new markets, invest in technology, strengthen sustainability practices, or create quality jobs, this programme offers valuable resources to accelerate long-term growth. With a streamlined online application process, no application fees, and decisions typically made within four to six weeks, eligible SMEs should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the 15 November 2026 deadline. Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY.
