title: Research Associate (PSA) – Inequality Between and Within Countries at UNU-WIDER in Finland (2026): Apply for an International Research Career in Development Economics
summary: The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) is inviting applications for the position of Research Associate (Personnel Service Agreement PSA) based in Helsinki, Finland . This is an excellent opportunity for researchers and development professionals who are passionate about inequality, economic development, poverty reduction, and evidence-based policymaking . The Research Associate will play an important role in maintaining internationally recognized inequality datasets, conducting high-quality research, and supporting the implementation of UNU-WIDER s Equality work programme. If you are seeking an international research position within the United Nations system where your work can contribute to global development policies and inequality research, this vacancy presents an outstanding career opportunity. Application Deadline: 22 July 2026 About UNU-WIDER The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) is one of the world s leading research institutions dedicated to producing policy-relevant research on development economics. Based in Helsinki, Finland , UNU-WIDER works closely with governments, researchers, international organizations, and policymakers to develop evidence-based solutions for reducing poverty, addressing inequality, and promoting sustainable economic growth worldwide. Its research informs international development policies and contributes to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Position Overview Job Title: Research Associate (PSA) – Inequality Between and Within Countries Duty Station: Helsinki, Finland Organization: United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) Contract Type: Personnel Service Agreement (PSA) What Will the Research Associate Do? The selected Research Associate will contribute significantly to UNU-WIDER s research activities focusing on inequality and development economics. Key responsibilities include: Maintaining and updating international inequality datasets. Conducting research related to inequality and development economics. Supporting the implementation of the Equality work programme. Assisting with research outputs and analytical work. Contributing to evidence generation that informs international development policy. Supporting collaborative research activities within UNU-WIDER. The position combines quantitative research, data management, and policy-oriented economic analysis. Why This Opportunity Is Important Growing inequality remains one of the greatest challenges facing countries around the world. Researchers working in this position will contribute to: Better understanding income and wealth inequality. Improving global inequality datasets. Supporting research used by governments and international organizations. Strengthening evidence-based policymaking. Advancing inclusive and sustainable development. Working with UNU-WIDER also provides valuable exposure to international research networks and multidisciplinary collaborations. Benefits of Working with UNU-WIDER Successful candidates can expect several professional advantages, including: Opportunity to work within the United Nations system. International research experience. Exposure to global development policy. Collaboration with leading economists and researchers. Opportunity to contribute to internationally recognized inequality databases. Professional networking with experts worldwide. Career development in development economics and international policy research. Work based in Helsinki, Finland, one of Europe s leading research hubs. Understanding UNU Contract Types According to UNU, there are three primary employment contract categories: Fixed-Term Staff Positions General Service National Officer Professional positions Personnel Service Agreement (PSA) Consultant Positions (CTC) Applicants are encouraged to review the contract information carefully before submitting an application. Recruitment Process Before applying, candidates are advised to review UNU s Recruitment and Selection Process to better understand: Application procedures Selection stages Recruitment policies Eligibility requirements Hiring timelines Understanding the recruitment process can help applicants prepare stronger applications. Why Consider a Career at UNU-WIDER? Joining UNU-WIDER offers much more than employment. Researchers become part of an internationally respected institution committed to solving some of the world s most pressing development challenges through rigorous academic research. Employees contribute directly to: Global economic research Poverty reduction initiatives Inequality analysis Development policy recommendations Sustainable Development Goals implementation This role is ideal for professionals who wish to combine academic excellence with real-world policy impact. Application Deadline Deadline: 22 July 2026 Interested candidates should submit their applications before the closing date, as late submissions may not be accepted. How to Apply Applicants should carefully review the complete vacancy announcement and submit their application through the official UNU Careers portal. UNU Careers: Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Research Associate (PSA) – Inequality Between and Within Countries at UNU-WIDER in Finland (2026): Apply for an International Research Career in Development Economics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands: Republic of Korea-Sponsored International Human Resources Career Opportunity
summary: The International Criminal Court (ICC) is inviting applications for the Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) position under the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme sponsored by the Republic of Korea . This prestigious international opportunity is based in The Hague, Netherlands , and offers young Korean professionals the chance to launch a rewarding career in international human resources within one of the world s leading judicial institutions. The position is hosted within the Human Resources Section of the Division of Management Services and provides successful candidates with practical experience in strategic human resources management, policy development, organizational development, recruitment, workforce planning, performance management, and HR operations. The appointment is initially for one year , with the possibility of extension in accordance with the JPO Memorandum of Understanding (MoU). Application Deadline: 12 July 2026 (midnight The Hague time) About the International Criminal Court (ICC) The International Criminal Court (ICC), headquartered in The Hague, Netherlands , is the world s first permanent international court established to prosecute individuals responsible for genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, and the crime of aggression. To effectively fulfill its global mandate, the ICC relies on highly qualified professionals from around the world who contribute expertise across legal, administrative, financial, human resources, and operational functions. The Human Resources Section plays a critical role in ensuring that the Court attracts, develops, and retains talented professionals capable of supporting its international mission. Position Overview Job Title: Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) Programme: Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Recruitment ID: 24421 Duty Station: The Hague, Netherlands Organizational Unit: Human Resources Section, Division of Management Services, Registry Contract Type: Junior Professional Officer Contract Duration: One-year appointment with the possibility of extension Minimum Net Annual Salary (Including Post Adjustment): €71,173 About the Human Resources Section The Human Resources Section provides centralized HR services throughout the Court while acting as a strategic advisor to management. The Section oversees every major aspect of human resource management, including: Strategic workforce planning Organizational design Recruitment and onboarding Payroll administration Compensation and benefits Pension administration Health insurance Staff development Learning and training programmes Performance management Succession planning Human resources policy development Conflict resolution and staff relations The Human Resources Section consists of two specialized units: 1. HR Operations Unit This unit manages operational HR activities such as: Recruitment Vacancy management Job classification Staff onboarding Payroll administration Compensation Benefits Health insurance Pension schemes Staff transfers Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) data management HR-related complaints and grievances 2. HR Organizational Development Unit This unit focuses on strategic human capital management by overseeing: Strategic workforce planning Talent management Organizational design Learning and development Performance management Leadership development Organizational effectiveness Main Responsibilities Working under the direct supervision of the Head of the Human Resources Operations Unit, the successful candidate will contribute to numerous strategic HR initiatives. Key responsibilities include: Supporting Human Resources Projects The Associate Human Resources Officer will: Support major HR projects. Assist in implementing HR-related Independent Expert Review (IER) recommendations. Monitor Registry Strategic Plan Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). HR Policy Research The officer will: Research HR regulations and best practices. Compare policies across similar international organizations. Prepare reports and recommendations. Support policy development and procedural improvements. HR Analysis Responsibilities include: Reviewing HR-related matters. Conducting detailed analysis. Preparing recommendations for HR management. Collaborating with HR specialists on technical matters. Policy Development The successful candidate will assist in: Developing HR policies. Monitoring implementation. Evaluating effectiveness. Ensuring compliance with organizational regulations. Performance Monitoring The role includes supporting the development and monitoring of: HR performance indicators Service Level Agreements Work plan trackers Management reporting tools Position Papers The officer will: Collect background information. Conduct preliminary legal and policy analysis. Assist in preparing position papers concerning HR regulations and interpretation. Internal Communication Responsibilities also include: Designing HR communication materials. Supporting internal awareness campaigns. Promoting HR initiatives and organizational objectives. Cross-Unit Support When required, the Associate Human Resources Officer will support different HR units with technical guidance and operational assistance. Other Duties Additional assignments may be delegated by management based on organizational priorities. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy both the ICC requirements and the Republic of Korea JPO Programme requirements. Republic of Korea JPO Requirements Applicants must: Be citizens of the Republic of Korea. Be fluent in Korean and English. Hold at least a Bachelor s degree. Meet one of the approved English language proficiency requirements: TOEFL iBT: minimum 100 TEPS: minimum 430 IELTS: minimum 7 TOEIC: minimum 900 Be no older than 32 years on 31 December 2026. Have completed Korean military service or qualify for exemption (male applicants). Applicants completing military service during the application year may also apply. Apply to a maximum of two Korean Ministry of Foreign Affairs JPO positions during the application year. Educational Qualifications Applicants should possess: An advanced university degree in: Human Resources Management Business Administration Public Administration Law Social Sciences Information Management Another relevant discipline OR A Bachelor s degree plus two additional years of qualifying professional experience. Professional Experience Applicants should have: At least two years of professional HR experience. Four years of experience if applying with a Bachelor s degree. Experience across various HR functions is desirable. Experience within organizations using the UN Common System is considered an advantage. Required Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Strong HR knowledge Excellent analytical and research skills Strategic planning ability Results-oriented mindset Excellent interpersonal communication Professional writing skills Cross-cultural communication abilities Respect for confidentiality Integrity and professionalism Ability to work under pressure Flexibility Teamwork Strong organizational skills Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in Korean. Fluency in English. Fluency in either English or French for Court operations. Working knowledge of the second Court language is desirable. Knowledge of Arabic, Chinese, Russian, or Spanish is an advantage. Documents Required Applicants must submit all documents in English through the ICC e-Recruitment System. Required documents include: Resident Register issued by the Korean Government Bachelor s and/or Master s degree certificates English language test certificate Employment certificates covering all listed professional experience Korean Military Service Certificate (where applicable) National Basic Livelihood Security Recipient Certificate (if applicable) Disability certificate (if applicable) Motivation letter in English Salary and Benefits The successful JPO will receive: Minimum annual net salary of €71,173 International professional experience Opportunity to work at the ICC Headquarters Exposure to international HR management Career development within the international civil service Mentorship and professional growth Possibility of contract extension ICC Leadership Competencies The ICC seeks professionals who demonstrate: Purpose Collaboration People Leadership Results Orientation ICC Core Competencies Successful candidates should embody: Dedication to the ICC mission and values Professionalism Teamwork Continuous learning Adaptability Effective interaction Achievement of objectives Why You Should Apply This JPO opportunity offers an exceptional pathway into the international civil service for young Korean professionals interested in human resources, organizational development, and global governance. Working at the ICC provides exposure to complex international HR operations while contributing to one of the world s most respected international institutions dedicated to justice and accountability. Candidates will gain invaluable experience working alongside professionals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds while building expertise applicable across the United Nations system and other international organizations. Application Deadline Deadline: 12 July 2026 (midnight The Hague time) Interested candidates should ensure that all required documents are prepared in English and submitted before the deadline. How to Apply Applications must be submitted electronically through the official ICC e-Recruitment System. Applicants are also encouraged to review information about the Republic of Korea JPO Programme through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs International Organizations Recruitment Center. Official ICC Careers Portal: https://careers.icc-cpi.int Republic of Korea JPO Programme: Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands: Republic of Korea-Sponsored International Human Resources Career Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UKRI Policy Internships 2026: Fully Supported Three-Month Policy Internship for UKRI-Funded Doctoral Students
summary: Are you a UK Research and Innovation (UKRI)-funded doctoral student looking to broaden your career experience beyond academia? The UKRI Policy Internships 2026 offer an exceptional opportunity to spend three months working within some of the UK s most influential government departments and policy organizations while continuing your doctoral studies. Designed specifically for doctoral researchers funded by UKRI research councils, the internship scheme enables participants to develop valuable policy, communication, and professional skills by translating academic research into practical evidence that informs government decision-making. Applications are now open, with internships expected to begin from January 2027 . Application Deadline: 8 September 2026 at 4:00 PM (UK Time). About the UKRI Policy Internships Scheme The UKRI Policy Internships Scheme provides doctoral students with first-hand experience of how research informs public policy across the United Kingdom. The programme bridges the gap between academia and government by allowing PhD students to work alongside policymakers, applying their research expertise to real-world policy challenges. Participants remain part of their doctoral studentship while gaining professional experience that can significantly enhance both academic and non-academic career prospects. Internship Duration Successful applicants will undertake: Duration: 3 months Expected Start: From January 2027 Internship Host Partners Internships are available across 16 influential policy organisations , including: Cabinet Office Department for Education Department for Energy Security and Net Zero Department for Work and Pensions Government Office for Science Home Office Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government And several other UK government and policy organisations. These placements give doctoral students direct exposure to how research supports evidence-based policymaking. Why Apply? The UKRI Policy Internships Scheme offers numerous professional and career benefits, including: Gain practical policy experience within the UK government. Learn how research influences public policy. Develop communication skills for non-academic audiences. Build relationships with policymakers and government professionals. Strengthen your CV with valuable public sector experience. Enhance your career prospects both inside and outside academia. Continue your doctoral training while undertaking the internship. Apply your research expertise to real policy challenges. Funding Funding arrangements vary depending on: Your UKRI research council. Your selected host organisation. Some research councils provide additional funding to support internship-related costs, while others require students to use funding available through their existing training grant. Who Can Apply? Applicants must: Be an existing UKRI-funded doctoral student. Hold funding from one of the following UKRI research councils: Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council (BBSRC) Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC) Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (ESRC) Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC) Medical Research Council (MRC) Natural Environment Research Council (NERC) Science and Technology Facilities Council (STFC) Application Timeline Activity Date Applications Open 2 June 2026 Application Deadline 8 September 2026 (4:00 PM UK Time) Shortlisting October 2026 Interviews October – November 2026 Results Announced November – December 2026 Internships Begin From January 2027 How to Apply Interested doctoral students should submit their applications through the official UKRI application portal before the deadline. Click here for more and to apply Why This Opportunity Matters For doctoral students interested in influencing public policy, this internship provides a rare opportunity to see how evidence shapes government decisions while building highly transferable skills. Whether you intend to pursue a career in academia, government, think tanks, international organisations, or the wider public sector, the UKRI Policy Internships Scheme offers invaluable practical experience and professional development that can set you apart in a competitive job market. Don t miss this opportunity to contribute your research expertise to some of the UK s leading policy institutions. Deadline: 8 September 2026 at 4:00 PM (UK Time). Click here for more global internship opportunities The post UKRI Policy Internships 2026: Fully Supported Three-Month Policy Internship for UKRI-Funded Doctoral Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Doctoral Student in IMER with a Focus on Survey Studies of Diversity and Inclusion at Malmö University, Sweden | Fully Funded PhD Opportunity 2027
summary: If you are passionate about migration studies, diversity, inclusion, social sciences, and quantitative research, Malmö University is offering an outstanding opportunity to pursue a fully funded doctoral studentship in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER) . This prestigious PhD position is hosted by the Department of Global Political Studies within the Faculty of Culture and Society and provides aspiring researchers with the chance to contribute to one of Sweden s leading migration research environments. The doctoral studentship is closely linked to the Migration Society 2.0 research programme and the internationally recognized Malmö Institute for Migration Studies (MIM) . Successful applicants will undertake an independent research project focused on survey studies of diversity and inclusion while participating in a vibrant interdisciplinary research community. The programme is designed for candidates who wish to build careers in academia, research, public policy, migration studies, international development, or social sciences. Application Deadline: 31 August 2026 About Malmö University Malmö University is one of Sweden s most innovative and internationally oriented higher education institutions. Located in the vibrant city of Malmö, the university brings together scholars, researchers, educators, and professionals from around the world to produce high-quality education and research that contributes to societal development. The university promotes collaboration across disciplines and values diversity, equality, innovation, and inclusion. Employees and doctoral students benefit from an international academic environment that encourages interdisciplinary research and strong engagement with society. About the Doctoral Position The Department of Global Political Studies (GPS) is seeking one doctoral student in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER) . The selected candidate will become part of a multidisciplinary research environment dedicated to understanding migration, ethnicity, integration, governance, politics, identity, and global social change. The doctoral project will focus on survey-based research exploring diversity and inclusion within Sweden through the Migration Society 2.0 programme. Research Focus: Diversity and Inclusion Survey Studies The doctoral research is part of the ambitious Migration Society 2.0 research programme, which seeks to deepen understanding of the increasingly diverse nature of Swedish society. The project investigates the concept of superdiversity , recognizing that modern migration has created societies characterized not only by increasing numbers of migrants but also by growing diversity in: Ethnicity National origin Migration pathways Legal status Gender Age Language Religion Social class Sexuality Transnational connections To better understand these societal transformations, Malmö University is developing a large-scale Diversity and Inclusion Survey , which will be repeated every two years to create an expanding database for future research. The survey will collect self-identified information relating to: Ethnicity Gender identity Religion Language Sexual orientation Social status Sense of belonging Attitudes toward other groups Perceptions of social institutions Experiences of inclusion and exclusion The successful doctoral student will design and conduct an original research project using data generated from this survey. Applicants are expected to submit a strong research proposal demonstrating theoretical originality, methodological rigor, and a clear vision for investigating diversity and inclusion through survey-based research. About the IMER Doctoral Programme The Doctoral Programme in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER) is an interdisciplinary programme examining migration and its consequences from structural, institutional, collective, and individual perspectives. Researchers within IMER explore topics including: International migration Refugee movements Integration Ethnic relations Identity Social inclusion Governance Citizenship Social inequality Cultural diversity Global mobility The programme integrates knowledge from numerous disciplines, including: Sociology Political Science Anthropology Cultural Geography Economics Economic History History Social Work Gender Studies Religious Studies Ethnology Cultural Studies Programme Structure The doctoral programme comprises 240 higher education credits , equivalent to four years of full-time study . The programme includes: 60 credits of doctoral coursework 180-credit independent doctoral dissertation Active participation in seminars Research meetings Departmental activities Collaboration with fellow researchers Public defense of the doctoral dissertation Doctoral students are expected to actively contribute to the academic environment at Malmö University and the Malmö Institute for Migration Studies. Employment Responsibilities The primary responsibility of the doctoral student is to pursue doctoral studies and conduct independent research. However, doctoral students may also contribute to: Teaching Educational activities Research assistance Academic administration These additional responsibilities cannot exceed 20% of a full-time position , in accordance with Sweden s Higher Education Ordinance. Research Environment The successful applicant will become part of the internationally renowned Malmö Institute for Migration Studies (MIM) . Established in 2007, MIM serves as a leading international research centre focusing on migration studies. It brings together senior and junior researchers while maintaining collaborations with scholars from around the world. The institute regularly hosts internationally recognized professors and promotes interdisciplinary research addressing migration, diversity, and inclusion. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy both the general and specific entry requirements for doctoral studies. General Entry Requirements Applicants must meet one of the following criteria: Hold a second-cycle (Master s) qualification. Have completed at least 240 higher education credits , including 60 credits at second-cycle level . Possess substantially equivalent qualifications obtained in Sweden or abroad. Specific Entry Requirements Applicants should possess academic qualifications equivalent to a second-cycle degree in: International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER), or Another discipline closely related to IMER. Relevant academic backgrounds include: Sociology Political Science Anthropology Cultural Geography Economics Economic History History Social Work Ethnology Religious Studies Gender Studies Cultural Studies Selection Criteria Admission is based on the applicant s ability to successfully complete doctoral education. Selection will consider: Independent analytical ability. Originality of previous academic work. Quality of the proposed research plan. Clarity of research questions. Theoretical understanding. Methodological competence. Scholarly maturity. Ability to conduct independent research. Additional advantages include: Knowledge of quantitative research methods. Statistical analysis skills. Survey methodology experience. Experience using statistical software. Database management experience. Data visualization expertise. Experience working with register or survey datasets. Application Documents Applicants must submit a complete application that includes: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Certified academic transcripts and diplomas Relevant certificates Research plan (maximum 3,000 words excluding references) Master s thesis or equivalent independent research work Personal letter (maximum 500 words) highlighting research experience, academic interests, and experience working with survey data Applications may be submitted in: English Swedish Any Nordic language Applicants are responsible for ensuring all required documents are translated where necessary. Employment Conditions The doctoral studentship offers: Four years of full-time doctoral study Fixed-term doctoral employment Salary according to Malmö University s doctoral salary agreement Possibility of teaching and academic duties (up to 20%) Access to Sweden s internationally recognized research environment Academic supervision Professional development opportunities International research collaboration The initial appointment is normally for one year and may be extended in accordance with Swedish regulations. Equality and Inclusion Malmö University actively promotes equal opportunities and an inclusive academic environment. The university encourages: Gender equality Equal opportunities for all applicants Support for doctoral students with disabilities Inclusive participation in research and education Applicants from an underrepresented gender may receive priority when candidates are equally qualified. International employees who do not speak Swedish are encouraged to learn the language over time to facilitate participation in university life. Residence Permit Information International students requiring a residence permit to study in Sweden must obtain a valid permit before commencing the programme. Failure to secure the required residence permit before the start date may result in the admission being withdrawn. Programme Start Date 1 February 2027 Why You Should Apply This doctoral studentship provides an exceptional opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research on migration, diversity, and inclusion while studying at one of Europe s leading migration research institutions. Benefits include: Fully funded doctoral employment Four years of funded PhD study Monthly doctoral salary Participation in an internationally renowned research centre Access to large-scale survey datasets Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration Academic supervision from leading migration scholars Strong international research networks Professional teaching and research experience Career preparation for academia, public policy, NGOs, and international organizations Apply Here Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than 31 August 2026. Candidates should ensure that all required documents are uploaded through Malmö University s recruitment system before the deadline. For more information, visit the official website. Discover more global PhD scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Doctoral Student in IMER with a Focus on Survey Studies of Diversity and Inclusion at Malmö University, Sweden | Fully Funded PhD Opportunity 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities
summary: The Global Civil Society Coalition for the UN Convention Against Corruption (UNCAC Coalition) has officially opened applications for two major international consultancy opportunities for professionals working in anti-corruption, governance, civil society engagement, advocacy, and human rights. The organization is currently recruiting: Regional Coordinator for Southwest Asia and North Africa (SWANA/MENA) Regional Coordinator for Latin America the Caribbean (LAC) These are remote, part-time consultancy roles designed for experienced professionals who are passionate about strengthening transparency, accountability, anti-corruption systems, and civil society participation across their respective regions. The opportunities provide selected consultants with the chance to collaborate with a globally recognized civil society network that works directly on issues related to the United Nations Convention against Corruption (UNCAC), the world’s only legally binding universal anti-corruption instrument. About the UNCAC Coalition The UNCAC Coalition is a global network consisting of more than 400 civil society organizations committed to promoting transparency, accountability, anti-corruption reforms, access to information, financial transparency, and human rights protections worldwide. The Coalition works to strengthen the implementation and monitoring of the United Nations Convention against Corruption by connecting civil society actors, advocacy groups, researchers, policy experts, and governance organizations from around the world. Its work includes: Advocacy and policy engagement Civil society coordination Monitoring UNCAC implementation Organizing trainings and workshops Producing research and reports Promoting transparency and public participation Supporting anti-corruption reforms globally The Coalition operates internationally and collaborates with stakeholders across governments, civil society organizations, regional institutions, and international organizations. Available Positions 1. Regional Coordinator – Southwest Asia North Africa (SWANA/MENA) The Coalition is seeking a consultant to coordinate outreach, communications, advocacy, and civil society engagement activities across the SWANA/MENA region. The selected consultant will help strengthen relationships with civil society organizations working on anti-corruption and governance issues while also supporting regional advocacy initiatives connected to UNCAC implementation. 2. Regional Coordinator – Latin America the Caribbean (LAC) The Coalition is also recruiting a consultant for the Latin America and Caribbean region to support regional anti-corruption engagement, civil society coordination, communications, networking, and research activities. This role focuses on expanding and strengthening the Coalition’s presence and collaboration across Latin America and the Caribbean. Position Type and Work Structure Both opportunities are: Remote positions Part-time consultancy roles International opportunities Long-term consultancy engagements Flexible working arrangements Estimated Weekly Workload Selected consultants are expected to work approximately: 6 to 10 hours per week Candidates who are flexible with their schedules and available for regional coordination activities are strongly preferred. Compensation and Benefits The consultancy roles offer: Estimated compensation of €20 per hour Potential for higher compensation depending on expertise and experience Flexible remote work arrangements International networking opportunities Opportunity to work with global anti-corruption experts and organizations Participation in regional and international advocacy activities Possible travel opportunities for UNCAC-related events International Conference Participation Selected consultants may also: Participate in the UNCAC Conference of the States Parties Receive approximately 10 days of covered travel expenses for major UNCAC events Represent the Coalition at regional anti-corruption meetings, depending on funding availability Main Responsibilities The Regional Coordinators will support a wide range of activities focused on strengthening anti-corruption civil society engagement in their regions. Key Responsibilities Include: Civil Society Network Development Expand and strengthen regional networks of civil society organizations Identify relevant anti-corruption organizations in the region Encourage organizations to join the Coalition Build stronger collaboration among regional members Regional Coordination Serve as a point of contact for regional civil society organizations Liaise between regional organizations and the Vienna Hub team Moderate regional email discussion groups Facilitate collaboration among members Events and Meetings Organize and moderate 4–5 online regional meetings annually Identify speakers and discussion themes Coordinate workshops and trainings Support virtual advocacy events Advocacy and Research Monitor anti-corruption developments in the region Conduct outreach to governments and civil society actors Support transparency and accountability initiatives Contribute to research and advocacy activities Content and Communications Draft briefing notes and reports Produce blog posts and written submissions Moderate regional discussions Share updates and advocacy materials Membership and UNCAC Engagement Support civil society participation in UNCAC review processes Encourage production of civil society parallel reports Help administer membership applications Promote transparency in UNCAC implementation reviews Required Qualifications SWANA/MENA Regional Coordinator Applicants must: Speak and write English and Arabic fluently Have strong knowledge of anti-corruption and governance issues in the region Possess at least 2 years of experience working with civil society organizations Be familiar with political and governance developments in the SWANA region Demonstrate strong writing, communication, and moderation skills Be self-motivated, organized, diplomatic, and reliable Preferably be based within the SWANA region Latin America Caribbean Regional Coordinator Applicants must: Speak and write English and Spanish fluently Have experience with civil society organizations in Latin America and the Caribbean Demonstrate strong understanding of anti-corruption and governance issues Be capable of coordinating regional advocacy activities Possess excellent communication and networking skills Show strong organizational and moderation abilities Preferably be based within the LAC region Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following: CV in English Maximum two pages PDF format No pictures One-page Motivation Letter in English PDF format Mention availability Mention potential conflicts of interest Writing Sample Maximum two pages In English Ideally existing professional work AI Disclosure Applicants must disclose any use of AI tools in preparing application materials Application Process Applicants must: Complete the official application form Email all required documents to: jobs@uncaccoalition.org Email Subject Lines “Regional Coordinator – SWANA Region” “Regional Coordinator – Latin America the Caribbean Region” Application Deadlines SWANA / MENA Position Deadline: Monday, 10 August 2026 Latin America Caribbean Position Deadline: Monday, 27 July 2026 (Midnight CEST) Starting Date Successful candidates are expected to begin: As soon as possible Why This Opportunity Matters These positions offer a valuable chance for professionals in governance, anti-corruption, advocacy, and civil society engagement to contribute to global transparency and accountability efforts. The selected consultants will work directly with one of the world’s leading anti-corruption civil society coalitions while helping shape regional advocacy, strengthen networks, and support international anti-corruption reforms. Professionals interested in governance, public policy, human rights, international development, and transparency initiatives will find these roles highly impactful and career-enhancing. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply for the SWANA Regional Coordinator Application HERE . Interested applicants can apply for the Latin American and the Caribbean Regional Coordinator HERE . Click HERE for more information on the UNCAC Coalition for SWANA Latin America 2026 job opportunities. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here .
